items.title: Matt Wright sued for negligence after airboat allegedly crashed into tree

children.content: A man who says he suffered a "psychiatric injury" after witnessing injuries sustained by his wife in a 2023 airboat crash is suing tour operator and Netflix star Matt Wright for negligence.

gallery.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a button-up shirt and jeans, walking towards the courthouse while holding his wife's hand.

avatar.alt: A journalist wearing a button-up shirt, with a neutral facial expression.

items.title: NT Labor MLA caught speeding multiple times, years after fatal crash conviction

children.content: Manuel Brown had his licence suspended for three months last October, which was not disclosed publicly at the time. The Arafura MLA also failed to disclose a conviction over a fatal crash when he was initially preselected for Labor in 2023.

image.altText: Man in suit sits on chair and puts pen in mouth

items.title: Second Mongols bikie to escape custody in the NT caught in eight minutes

children.content: A "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw bikie gang has been arrested after fleeing custody at Royal Darwin Hospital — the second time a Mongols member has fled custody in the Northern Territory this month.

image.altText: The side door of a NT Police car.

items.title: One CDU employee has been back paid $242k amid $4m stolen wages bill

children.content: Charles Darwin University (CDU) has been directed to back pay more than $4 million in wages after it was found to have underpaid more than 800 professional and academic staff members between 2016 and 2022.

items.title: Ex-CEO vindicated in $2.3 million court ruling against space firm

children.content: Once a promising space start-up chief executive, Carley Scott was terminated from her job and left millions out of pocket. Now she's finally found vindication, with a court ruling in her favour.

image.altText: A woman with a brown ponytail wearing hi-vis smiles at a worksite

items.title: NT Labor leader defends office culture in 'unique' political workplace

children.content: Northern Territory Opposition Leader Selena Uibo has defended her handling of workplace misconduct allegations in her office, saying it is a "highly-charged political environment that is unique and unlike most workplaces".

image.altText: A woman, looking serious, standing in front of a few media microphones at an outdoor press conference.

items.title: Government responses criticised two months on from Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

image.altText: A pink flower resting on the ground, colourful candles are burning nearby.

items.title: $800k for mediation not spent prior to fatal stabbing

children.content: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

image.altText: A multi-colour flower memorial resting on the edge of a curb in front of a home, with orange paint.

items.title: Award-winning artist accused of copying second artwork

children.content: Australian artist Jane Allan, revealed to have previously copied another painter's work, is being accused of a second case of imitation.

image.altText: An artwork featuring a human-like outline.

items.title: Christian Brothers spending $1.7m a week on abuse cases

children.content: The Congregation of Christian Brothers Oceania Province has revealed it was seeking to sell off its 36 properties to pay abuse victims, who would be treated as creditors.

image.altText: A wooden cross at the top of a building with blue sky behind it.

items.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson taken offline

children.content: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

image.altText: Two men in black on a street.

items.title: Will Congress demanding US military halt action in Iran end the war?

children.content: The US Senate approved a war powers resolution seeking to block military action against Iran on Tuesday. But don't expect the war to end.

image.altText: The US Capitol Hill building seen at sunset, framed by dark silhouettes of trees and parked cars

items.title: Socceroos should make 'harsh' call for final group game, Schwarzer says

children.content: As the Socceroos prepare to face Paraguay in their final FIFA World Cup group game, Aussie great Mark Schwarzer says now is the time to bring captain Mat Ryan back into the team.

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play Paraguay and what time is the game?

children.content: The Socceroos have one more match in the group stage as they look to make it to the knockout stages of the World Cup. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Irankunda slides along a green pitch on his knees.

articles.title: 'Doesn't seem right': Why Socceroos won't play for a draw against Paraguay

children.content: A draw would be enough for the Socceroos to progress from their World Cup group, but according to defender Jason Geria, playing for anything less than a win does not sit right with this group.

image.altText: Jordan Bos #5 of Australia is tackled by Hakan Calhanoglu #10 of Turkiye

articles.title: Quick hits: Trump's special job and commentator banned for outburst

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo makes a bit of World Cup history as Gianni Infantino offers an update on the future of the hydration breaks and Donald Trump is given role at this year's final. Here are today's quick hits.

items.title: UN maritime body starts enacting plan to move sailors out of Gulf

children.content: An evacuation plan to enable hundreds of ships to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is being enacted, according to the UN's International Maritime Organization.

image.altText: Lots of small boats sit on the water of the  Strait of Hormuz. Huge brown mountains loom in the background.

items.title: Tech billionaire accused of assaulting partner, cutting $66k Birkin bag with scissors

children.content: A billionaire tech investor accused of assaulting his former partner and stealing expensive gifts he had bought her has his bail varied so he can visit his mother in Sydney.

image.altText: A man in a suit walking down a street as two reporters point microphones towards him

items.title: Bird flu has arrived in Australia

children.content: After years of waiting, the deadly H5N1 strain of bird flu has finally arrived in Australia.
Two birds found on the southern coast of Western Australia near Esperance have been confirmed as infected, but there is no evidence so far of a mass-mortality event.
So, what actually is H5N1, and why does it matter?

items.title: Wayne Bennett rubbishes 'myths' around South Sydney's injuries

children.content: Not for the first time, South Sydney is counting the cost of an outsized injury toll, with questions being asked about their training surface.

image.altText: A rugby league player in red and green is on his haunches on the field.

items.title: Heartbroken Daryl Braithwaite will miss everything about performing

children.content: The ARIA Hall of Fame inductee speaks emotionally to the ABC about his decision to give up live performances, saying it has broken his heart to see how appreciative people have been.

items.title: 'Really dangerous': Social media trend mummymaxxing explained

children.content: Also known as mommymaxxing and mommaxxing — depending on which internet rabbit hole you're going down — mummymaxxing focuses on optimising motherhood and experts warn it could be harmful.

items.title: Man, 78, dies after 'amateur-built replica Spitfire' crashes in WA

children.content: Police say a light plane crashed in Malabaine on Wednesday morning, with the pilot and sole occupant of the aircraft found dead at the scene.

items.title: Murray-Darling Basin communities call for balance in review

children.content: Communities across the Murray-Darling Basin are calling for a better balance, as irrigators say water buybacks are hurting their communities.

items.title: ICAC told favoured candidates got questions before uni's job interviews

children.content: An MND expert says the registry is a first step to help identify what causes the incurable disease and how it could be stopped.

items.title: Court hears girl was groomed to repopulate 'post-apocalyptic' world

children.content: A complainant has told a court that alleged NSW South Coast cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm groomed her for more than a decade.

items.title: Family shares significant update on Sydney shark attack victim's condition

children.content: A Sydney mother mauled by a shark at Coogee Beach almost a fortnight ago has spoken her first words after briefly waking from an induced coma.

items.title: Family left 'shattered', 'traumatised' by woman's murder, court told

items.title: Police release video of Townsville gym shooting and shop fire

items.title: Drug charges laid after women allegedly try to evade police after exiting Spirit ferry

children.content: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

items.title: What stolen party funds bought Nicola Sturgeon's husband

children.content: Court documents have revealed what former Scottish first minister Nicola Sturgeon's husband Peter Murrell bought with funds he embezzled from the party over 12 years.

items.title: DRC Ebola outbreak grows to become one of worst recorded

children.content: There are now more than 1,000 confirmed cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo and 267 people have died, local officials say.

items.title: IVF add-ons are common in Australia, but there's no proof most work

children.content: Experts urge clinics and specialists to "carefully consider" offering IVF add-on treatments as they can be seen as "implicit endorsement of benefit" when there's little evidence most work.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: Port Moresby's water supply network showing 'signs of imminent failure'

children.content: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

image.altText: Water bursts from two points in a long concrete flume.
